### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Documented search:** I actively checked (1) the table column schema for shape consistency with the existing methodology — Static + Dynamic = Total math reconciles (52718 + 6869 = 59587 ✓), Cycle Type "1" matches the "1 or N" column header, Reviewer column points at the actual cycle-1 reviewer; (2) the row's empirical anchor — PR #10563 cycle 1 was authored by Gemini and reviewed by me earlier today, the `Reviewer @neo-opus-ada` cell matches; (3) post-rebase contamination — `git diff origin/dev...HEAD --stat` shows exactly 1 file, 1 line, no resources/content sync snapshots remain. Found one Polish-level observation, no blocking concerns.

**Polish-level observation (non-blocking, optional follow-up):** the baseline file is named `pr-review-baseline-2026-04.md` (April 2026), but today is 2026-05-01 and subsequent cycles will land in May/June. Two valid interpretations:
- (a) File name is a STARTING-month canonical anchor — the baseline began in April when Sub-1 was framed; subsequent months continue in the same file. Defensible if documented.
- (b) Intended to be `2026-05` for the May-onward cycles. If so, rename for accuracy.

If interpretation (a) is correct, a one-line note at the top of the file ("Tracker spans April 2026 onwards — file name reflects measurement start date") would close the ambiguity. Not gating; ship-as-is is fine if (a) was deliberate.
